info.title: BusinessApi
info.description:
- return HttpOK(HttpCode-200) when process work successfully
- return HttpException(400) when request is invalid (bad request), or other errors
- return HttpException(401) when access_token or api_key is invalid or expired
- return HttpException(403) when permission to access source is denied
- return HttpException(404) when source not found, or not exist
- return HttpException(405) when method not allowed, or not supported
- return HttpException(413) when request payload is too large
- return HttpException(415) when request content type is not supported
- return HttpException(422) when request is invalid (unprocessable entity)
- return HttpException(429) when too many requests in a period of time
- return HttpException(500) when something wrong in internal server
- return HttpException(502) when bad gateway, or gateway timeout
- return HttpException(503) when service unavailable, or maintenance
- return HttpException(504) when gateway timeout, or service timeout

post.description:
create access_token and refresh_token based on OAuth2PasswordRequestForm
- **username**: value of email, phone or username
- **password**: value of password, plain text or hash value
refresh access_token of current user based on refresh_token
send a code to email for signup or reset, return token with code
verify code & token from send-code, and create user or reset password

get.description: get current user schema, return user schema

patch.description:
Update basic user information

This endpoint allows users to update their basic profile information such as nickname
and avatar. It strictly separates user basic attributes from organization-specific
configuration.

Parameters:
- user_id: The ID of the user to update
- user_update: A UserUpdate object containing fields to update
  - nickname: Optional new display name for the user
  - avatar: Optional new avatar URL for the user

Permissions:
- Users can only update their own information
- Authentication is required

Returns:
- UserSchema: The updated user object

Raises:
- 404 Not Found: If the user ID does not exist or does not match the current user

get.description: get role schema list based on object_class, return role schema list

post.description:
create organization model, return organization schema
:note: use get_current_token_info instead of get_current_token_info_o

get.description: get organization schema by organization_id, return organization schema

patch.description:
update organization model by organization_id, return organization schema
:raise: 403 Forbidden if current_user.organization_role != "admin"

delete.description: Delete organization model by organization_id, should only be accessible by internal API_KEY (currently not implemented)

post.description:
Search for users in an organization with pagination.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Accepts filter, search and pagination parameters in request body
- Supports filtering by user status, role, and job function
- Supports searching by display_name prefix
- Returns paginated results with next_token for subsequent requests

Job function filtering:
- Set job_function_id to a valid ID to find users with that job function
- Set job_function_id to null explicitly to find users without any job function
- Omit job_function_id to not filter by job function at all

Example request body:
```json
{
  "filter": {
    "status": "ACTIVE",
    "role_id": "role_123",
    "job_function_id": "jf_456"
  },
  "search": {
    "display_name": "john"
  },
  "limit": 25,
  "next_token": "eyJsYXN0X2lkI..."
}
```

Example request to find users without job function:
```json
{
  "filter": {
    "job_function_id": null
  },
  "limit": 25
}
```

Returns:
    PagedUserListResponse: List of organization users with pagination metadata
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not an admin

get.description:
Get a paginated list of users in an organization without filtering.
Use POST /search endpoint instead for advanced search and filtering.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Returns a simple paginated list of all users in the organization
- Does not support filtering or searching

Get a specific organization user by ID.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Returns detailed information about a specific user in the organization
- Includes job function information if assigned

Returns:
    OrganizationUserSchema: The organization user details
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not an admin
    HTTPException: 404 if user not found in the organization

403.description: Permission denied - User is not an admin

404.description: Not found - User not found in this organization

patch.description:
Update an organization user.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Can update a user's role and job function
- Only allows updates for users with ACTIVE or INVITED status
- Validates that the role exists if changed
- Validates that the job function exists if changed
- Returns the updated user with job function details

Updatable fields:
- role_id: Change the user's role in the organization
- job_function_id: Update the user's job function
  - Set to a valid job function ID to assign/change job function
  - Set explicitly to null to remove/unassign job function
  - Omit the field to leave job function unchanged

Returns:
    OrganizationUserSchema: The updated organization user
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not an admin
    HTTPException: 404 if user not found in the organization
    HTTPException: 400 if role or job function is invalid
    HTTPException: 400 if user has INACTIVE status

403.description: Permission denied - User is not an admin

404.description: Not found - User not found in this organization

400.description: Bad request - Invalid role, job function, or user status

delete.description:
Remove a user from the organization.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- For ACTIVE users: Performs a soft delete by changing the status to INACTIVE
- For INVITED users: Hard deletes the organization user record and cancels associated invitation
- For INACTIVE users: Does nothing (idempotent operation)
- Prevents an admin from removing themselves

Returns:
    None: 204 No Content on successful removal
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not an admin
    HTTPException: 404 if user not found in the organization
    HTTPException: 400 if attempting to remove self

403.description: Permission denied - User is not an admin

404.description: Not found - User not found in this organization

400.description: Bad request - Cannot remove self from organization

post.description:
Reactivate a previously removed (inactive) user.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Changes a user's status from INACTIVE to ACTIVE
- Returns the updated user details
- Only works for users with INACTIVE status

Returns:
    OrganizationUserSchema: The reactivated organization user
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not an admin
    HTTPException: 404 if user not found in the organization
    HTTPException: 400 if user is not in INACTIVE status

403.description: Permission denied - User is not an admin

404.description: Not found - User not found in this organization

400.description: Bad request - User is not in INACTIVE status

post.description:
Create a new invitation for a user to join an organization.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Validates that the invitee is not already a member of the organization
- Checks for existing active invitations to prevent duplicates
- Creates an invitation record with a 7-day expiration period
- Sends an invitation email (when implemented)

Returns:
    OrganizationInvitationSchema: The created invitation
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not an admin
    HTTPException: 400 if invitee is already a member or email is invalid
    HTTPException: 409 if an active invitation already exists

403.description: Permission denied - User is not an admin

400.description: Bad request - User is already a member of this organization or invalid email

409.description: Conflict - An active invitation already exists for this email

get.description:
Get a specific invitation by ID.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Validates that the invitation exists and belongs to the organization

Returns:
    OrganizationInvitationSchema: The requested invitation
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not an admin
    HTTPException: 404 if invitation is not found in the organization

403.description: Permission denied - User is not an admin

404.description: Not found - Invitation not found in this organization

post.description:
Resend an invitation email.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Validates that the invitation exists and belongs to the organization
- Checks that the invitation is still in pending status
- Updates the expiration date if the invitation was expired
- Resends the invitation email (when implemented)

Returns:
    Resp: Success message
    
Raises:
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not an admin
    HTTPException: 404 if invitation is not found in the organization
    HTTPException: 400 if invitation is not in pending status

403.description: Permission denied - User is not an admin

404.description: Not found - Invitation not found in this organization

400.description: Bad request - Only pending invitations can be resent

post.description:
Search for job functions in an organization with pagination.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Accepts search and pagination parameters in request body
- Supports searching job functions by name prefix
- Returns paginated results with next_token for subsequent requests

Example request body:
```json
{
  "search": "manager",
  "limit": 25,
  "next_token": "eyJsYXN0X2lkI..."
}
```

Returns:
    RespJobFunctionList: List of job functions with pagination metadata

get.description:
Get a paginated list of job functions for an organization without search capability.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Returns a simple paginated list of all job functions in the organization
- Supports pagination but does not support search
- For search functionality, use the POST /search endpoint

post.description:
Create a new job function in the organization.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Validates that the job function name is unique within the organization
- Validates that the job function name is at least 3 characters long
- Handles potential race conditions with database constraints

Returns:
    JobFunctionSchema: The created job function

Raises:
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not an admin
    HTTPException: 400 if job function with same name already exists
    HTTPException: 400 if job function name is less than 3 characters

patch.description:
Update an existing job function by ID.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Validates that the job function exists in the organization
- Validates that the job function name is at least 3 characters long if provided
- If updating the name, ensures the new name is unique
- Handles potential race conditions

Returns:
    JobFunctionSchema: The updated job function

Raises:
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not an admin
    HTTPException: 404 if job function is not found
    HTTPException: 400 if updating to a name that already exists
    HTTPException: 400 if job function name is less than 3 characters

delete.description:
Delete a job function by ID.

This endpoint:
- Requires admin permissions
- Validates that the job function exists in the organization
- Prevents deletion if the job function is in use by any users

Returns:
    None: 204 No Content on successful deletion

Raises:
    HTTPException: 403 if user is not an admin
    HTTPException: 404 if job function is not found
    HTTPException: 400 if job function is in use by users

get.description:
Verify an invitation token and return information about the invitation

This endpoint:
- Validates the invitation token
- Returns essential information about the invitation and organization
- Does not require authentication (accessible to unauthenticated users)
- Used when a user clicks on an invitation link to check if it's valid
- Implements rate limiting to prevent abuse

Args:
    token: The invitation token (UUID) to verify
    session: Database session
    rd_conn: Redis connection for rate limiting

Returns:
    InvitationVerifyResponse: Essential details about the invitation and organization

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if invitation not found
    HTTPException: 400 if invitation is not pending or has expired
    HTTPException: 429 if rate limit exceeded
    HTTPException: 500 if unexpected error occurs

404.description: Not found - Invitation or organization not found

400.description: Bad request - Invitation is not pending or has expired

429.description: Too many requests - Rate limit exceeded

post.description:
Accept an invitation to join an organization.

This endpoint:
- Requires user authentication
- Validates the invitation token
- Checks that the current user's email matches the invitation email
- Enforces that users can only be active in one organization at a time
- Handles idempotent requests (accepting the same invitation multiple times)
- Updates the organization user record to link it to the current user
- Updates the invitation status to "accepted"
- Implements rate limiting to prevent abuse

The operation is performed in a transaction to ensure data consistency:
- Both the organization user and invitation records must be updated together
- Either both succeed or both fail

Args:
    token: The invitation token (UUID) to accept
    current_user: The authenticated user (from access token)
    session: Database session
    rd_conn: Redis connection for rate limiting

Returns:
    Resp: Success message

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if invitation not found
    HTTPException: 400 if invitation is not pending or has expired
    HTTPException: 400 if user email doesn't match invitation email
    HTTPException: 400 if user is already a member of another organization
    HTTPException: 429 if rate limit exceeded
    HTTPException: 500 if unexpected error occurs

404.description: Not found - Invitation or organization not found

400.description: Bad request - Invitation expired, email mismatch, or user already in an organization

429.description: Too many requests - Rate limit exceeded

post.description:
get status of database and redis
session: Session = Depends(get_session)
send email by background tasks

test request, response, cookie and header, especially secret_key and client_id
recommended to use Depends(APIKeyHeader(name="Authorization") to get secret_key and client_id
-H 'Authorization: Bearer xxxxxxxx' -H 'ClientID: xxxxxxxx'
test sign value of payload, sign value from header

InvitationVerifyResponse.title: InvitationVerifyResponse
InvitationVerifyResponse.description:
Response model for invitation verification

Contains only essential details about the invitation and organization
to display to the user before they accept the invitation.

UserUpdate.title: UserUpdate
UserUpdate.description:
User Update Schema

Contains only basic user information that can be updated, such as
nickname and avatar. Organization-specific information should be
handled through the OrganizationUser schema.
